stories.summary: Palestinian couples gathered for a mass wedding ceremony in Gaza organised amid rubble and displacement.

stories.headline: Video: Philippine senator flees ICC arrest over role in drug war
stories.summary: Philippines Senator Ronald Dela Rosa has taken refuge in the country’s parliament, as police sought to detain him.

stories.headline: Lamine Yamal’s Palestine flag wave hailed by fans, activists and athletes
stories.summary: Palestinians in Gaza, football fans from around the world and activists laud Barca and Spain footballer.

stories.headline: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
stories.summary: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

stories.headline: Pakistan struck a rehab centre and killed 269 Afghans. Their families want to know why
stories.summary: There are calls to investigate the 16 March airstrike - which the UN says likely killed more - as a war crime.

stories.headline: Dimon Says Effects of Iran War Are Getting More Serious Each Day
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon says the effects of the Iran war are getting more serious each day. He also says the wealthier American consumer "is spending like they should." He speaks to Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the bank’s annual Global Markets Conference in Paris.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Hegseth faces bipartisan scrutiny over mounting Iran war costs
stories.summary: The defense secretary appears before Congress as a ceasefire with Tehran falters and lawmakers question the administration’s plan for a massive hike in military spending.

stories.headline: At least nine killed in explosion at Pakistan market
stories.summary: At least nine people were killed and some 30 injured in a blast at a market in the Pakistani town of Sarai Naurang.

stories.headline: At least nine people killed in explosion at Pakistan market
stories.summary: About 30 people wounded in blast in town of Sarai Naurang, in Lakki Marwat district, northwestern Pakistan.

stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.headline: Food prices could skyrocket, if no measures taken to tackle fertilizer crisis, farmer unions warn
stories.summary: The fertilizer crisis caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is tripling costs for farmers. “Less fertilizers means less production for wheat. That’s the basis for bread,” warns COPA President, Massimiliano Giansanti.

stories.headline: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
stories.summary: Yoweri Museveni, 81, won disputed elections in January to extend his 40-year rule.

stories.headline: EU needs to delay social media access for children - von der Leyen
stories.summary: She told an EU summit that an expert panel was due to come up with steps on how to protect minors online by July.

stories.headline: Trump ally Kari Lake tapped to be US ambassador to Jamaica
stories.summary: Lake thanked President Trump for nominating her, saying on social media that Jamaica "is a country I know very well, full of incredible people".

stories.headline: Dimon Says JPMorgan Would Scrap New UK HQ Over Higher Bank Taxes
stories.summary: Jamie Dimon warned the UK that any move to hike taxes on banks in the event Keir Starmer is replaced as the UK’s prime minster would see JPMorgan Chase & Co. scrap plans to invest billions in a new London headquarters in Canary Wharf.

stories.headline: Kari Lake, Who Led Effort to Shutter Voice of America, Is Named Ambassador to Jamaica
stories.summary: Ms. Lake tried and failed to fire hundreds of Voice of America journalists and to cut funding for other federally funded news groups like Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty.

stories.headline: Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang Will Plead Guilty to Working as Chinese Agent
stories.summary: Eileen Wang, who resigned Monday as the mayor of Arcadia, Calif., published propaganda on a purported news site under direction from Chinese officials, prosecutors said.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Clears Path for Alabama to Use New Voting Map
stories.summary: A majority of the justices sided with Alabama in a move that could speed up efforts to put in place a congressional district map that would eliminate a majority-Black district.

stories.headline: Virginia Democrats appeal to U.S. Supreme Court to save new House maps
stories.summary: Some top Virginia Democrats express little hope that the appeal will affect this November’s congressional elections and instead will focus on running in existing districts.

stories.summary: Vietnam’s state oil company urged the US to let a supertanker pass through an American naval blockade outside of the Persian Gulf, saying the shipment is critical to the Asian country’s economy.

stories.headline: Amazon Sells Swiss Franc Bond Offering in Record Six Parts
stories.summary: Amazon.com Inc. sold its first Swiss franc bonds across a record six tranches, as Big Tech looks beyond its regular debt markets to raise funds.

stories.headline: Vodafone Shares Plunge as German Client Losses Accelerate
stories.summary: Vodafone Group Plc shares fell the most in three years after the telecommunications operator lost clients in its key German market last quarter due to intense competition and a push to boost revenue per user.

stories.headline: As he prepares to meet Xi, Trump no longer pushes for change in China’s system
stories.summary: In his first term, the president demanded fundamental shifts in China’s economic model. Now, he has copied parts of how Beijing does business.

stories.headline: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call
stories.summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he would not resign on Monday evening after days of speculation.

stories.headline: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
stories.summary: The EU's foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences", while Israel branded the move "arbitrary".

stories.headline: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader's descendants

stories.headline: Advocaat open to return as Curacao boss resigns
stories.summary: Dick Advocaat could make a surprise return as head coach of the Curacao national team after his successor Fred Rutten resigned from the role just a month out from their World Cup debut.

stories.headline: Petrobras Misses Estimates Despite War-Driven Oil Rally
stories.summary: Brazil’s state-controlled oil company Petrobras fell short of earnings estimates as it held domestic gasoline prices stable during the war-driven price surge.

stories.headline: Virginia Officials Ask Supreme Court to Restore Voting Map Drawn by Democrats
stories.summary: State officials asked the justices to overturn a Virginia Supreme Court decision that struck down a congressional map, a major defeat for Democrats.

stories.headline: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
stories.summary: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.

stories.headline: Confident in China’s power, Xi is ready to host an unpredictable Trump
stories.summary: Holding no illusions about making lasting deals at this week’s summit, China’s leader looks to project Beijing as an alternative to U.S. volatility on the world stage.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Continues Access to Abortion Pill by Mail, for a Few Days
stories.summary: Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. has paused until at least Thursday a federal appeals court ruling against the Food and Drug Administration that would restrict access by mail to mifepristone.

stories.headline: His father had just been buried. Then West Bank settlers forced him to dig up the body

stories.summary: Though Vijay campaigned only briefly, his fan club-turned-digital “warriors” amplified him online tirelessly.

stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to trade fire despite a ceasefire deal being announced last month.

stories.headline: Steve Rosenberg: This year's Victory Day parade in Moscow felt very different
stories.summary: The BBC's Russia editor reports from a Red Square with none of the military hardware which the Kremlin normally showcases to project power internationally.

stories.headline: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.summary: A former ally of Mamata Banerjee, the BJP leader will be West Bengal’s next chief minister.

stories.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

stories.headline: Wall Street Poised for Bonus Increases in ‘Year of the Bank’
stories.summary: Wall Street bonuses are projected to jump for the third year in a row as market volatility fuels trading demand and dealmaking makes its long-awaited comeback. For investment bankers who advise corporate clients on deals, incentive pay is poised to be up 10% to 20% or more from a year earlier, according to Johnson Associates. Katherine Doherty has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Exxon and Chevron have benefited less than their European rivals
